zh.lifeSummary: 當William Humphrey Grizzell於1600年出生在英國薩默塞特龐迪斯福德 帕克時，他的父親William P Grizzell26歲，母親Nancy Tribb30歲。他於1624年與Lucretia Christine Ripley在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州艾可拉帕韓諾可結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1636年6月2日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州艾可拉帕韓諾可享年36歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州。

nameMeanings.name: Grizzell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Grizzle   2, 3. Compare   Grizzel  .
Variant of   Grizzle   1, a surname of French origin.

variants.name: Grizzle
meanings: Americanized form of French or Swiss French Grisel ‘gray’ or of its rare variant Grizel.
English (Hertfordshire): nickname from Middle English (Old French) grisel ‘gray(-haired)’, also used as a term for an old man.

English: occasionally perhaps from the Middle English female personal name Grisil(d) (perhaps of ancient Germanic origin), but it is attested very rarely in medieval England and probably too late to be the source of a surname in the English Midlands. It was made familiar through the heroine of Chaucer's Clerk's Tale, a re-writing of an Italian folk tale in versions by Boccaccio and Petrarch, but the personal name is earlier than the publication of Chaucer's work.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Humphrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

variants.name: Humphry

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: The Virginia Company founded the first permanent English colony at Jamestown in 1607. One of the original 13 states (it entered the Union in 1788). Virginia holds an important place in American history, as it was home to many of the founding fathers, including George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, James Madison, George Mason, and Patrick Henry. Four of Americas first five presidents were Virginians. During the Civil War, Richmond, Virginias capital, was the capital of the Confederacy. Today, Virginia is a popular tourist spot where people can visit historic places such as Alexandria, Williamsburg, and Mount Vernon, George Washingtons estate.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When William Humphrey Grizzell was born in 1600, in Poundisford Park, Somerset, England, his father, William P Grizzell, was 26 and his mother, Nancy Tribb, was 30. He married Lucretia Christine Ripley in 1624, in Rappahannock, Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 2 June 1636, in Rappahannock, Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 36, and was buried in Virginia, British Colonial America.

ko.Rappahannock, Essex, Virginia, British C: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 에세스 랩아해노c크
ko.Rappahannock, Virginia, British Colonial: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 드 랩아해노c크
ko.lifeSummary: William Humphrey Grizzell(이)가 1600년 영국 서머세트 파우디스퍼드 박에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William P Grizzell(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Nancy Tribb(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1624년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 에세스 랩아해노c크에서 Lucretia Christine Ripley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1636년 6월 2일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 에세스 랩아해노c크에서 36세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Humphrey Grizzell nasceu em 1600, em Poundisford Park, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, William P Grizzell, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Nancy Tribb, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Lucretia Christine Ripley em 1624, em Rappahannock, Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 2 de junho de 1636, em Rappahannock, Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America, com 36 anos, e foi sepultado em Virginia, British Colonial America.

ru.Rappahannock, Virginia, British Colonial: олд раппаханнокк, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Humphrey Grizzell родился в 1600 г. в местности поундисфорд парк, сомирсет, Англия, его отцу по имени William P Grizzell было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Nancy Tribb, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Lucretia Christine Ripley в 1624 г. в местности раппаханнокк, эссекс, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 2 июня 1636 г. в местности раппаханнокк, эссекс, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 36 лет и был похоронен в местности Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Humphrey Grizzell nació en 1600, en Poundisford Park, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, William P Grizzell, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Nancy Tribb, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Lucretia Christine Ripley en 1624, en Rappahannock, Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 2 de junio de 1636, en Rappahannock, Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America, a la edad de 36 años, y fue sepultado en Virginia, British Colonial America.
